# 📚 SMART RAILWAY - COMPLETE DOCUMENTATION INDEX

## Your Questions Answered ✅

### Question: "Are SECRET_KEY, JWT_SECRET_KEY, CSRF_HEADER_NAME, SESSION_SECRET generated by system or do I need to generate them?"

**ANSWER:**
- **SECRET_KEY** - ✅ YOU GENERATE using `secrets.token_hex(32)`
- **SESSION_SECRET** - ✅ YOU GENERATE using `secrets.token_hex(64)`
- **JWT_SECRET_KEY** - ✅ YOU GENERATE using `secrets.token_hex(64)`
- **CSRF_HEADER_NAME** - ❌ USE DEFAULT `X-CSRF-Token` (no generation needed)

## 🔐 Key Concepts Explained

### Session ID
- **Definition:** A unique 43-character cryptographic token
- **Purpose:** Identifies authenticated user without exposing password
- **Storage:** HttpOnly cookie + database
- **Duration:** 24 hours absolute, 6 hours idle
- **See:** SESSION_ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md

### CSRF Token
- **Definition:** Prevents cross-site request forgery attacks
- **Purpose:** Ensures requests come from your app
- **Storage:** Frontend localStorage + database
- **Validation:** Checked on POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH requests
- **See:** SESSION_ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE.md

### Secret Keys
- **Definition:** Cryptographically random strings
- **Purpose:** Signing/encryption of tokens
- **Generation:** Using Python `secrets` module
- **Security:** CRITICAL - never share or hardcode
- **See:** ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES_GUIDE.md

### OTP (One-Time Password)
- **Definition:** 6-digit code sent to user's email
- **Purpose:** Email verification during registration
- **Expiry:** 15 minutes (configurable)
- **Attempts:** Max 3 before invalidation
- **See:** CATALYST_EMAIL_SETUP.md

```
F:\New Railway Project\

📄 DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md                      ← This file

└─ functions/smart_railway_app_function/
   ├─ .env                                     ← Your configuration
   ├─ .env.example                             ← Template
   ├─ app.py
   ├─ config.py
   ├─ services/
   │  └─ otp_service.py                       ← OTP implementation

   │  └─ otp_register.py                      ← Registration routes
   └─ docs/
      ├─ SESSION_MANAGEMENT_GUIDE.md          ← Session details
      ├─ CATALYST_EMAIL_SETUP.md              ← Email setup
      └─ SESSION_SCHEMA.md                    ← Database schema

└─ railway-app/
   └─ src/
      ├─ context/
      │  └─ SessionAuthContext.jsx            ← Auth context
      ├─ components/
      │  └─ OTPVerification.jsx               ← OTP UI
      ├─ pages/auth/
      │  └─ AuthPage.jsx                      ← Registration page
      └─ services/
         └─ sessionApi.js                     ← API client
```

## ✅ Complete Setup Checklist

Before starting your application:

**Generate Keys:**
- [ ] Run Python script to generate SECRET_KEY
- [ ] Run Python script to generate SESSION_SECRET
- [ ] Run Python script to generate JWT_SECRET_KEY

**Create Configuration:**
- [ ] Create `.env` file in `functions/smart_railway_app_function/`
- [ ] Paste all three generated keys
- [ ] Set CATALYST_FROM_EMAIL to verified email
- [ ] Set ADMIN_SETUP_KEY to strong password
- [ ] Set other variables (use defaults if unsure)

**Verify Setup:**
- [ ] Run verification script to confirm keys are set
- [ ] Verify email in Zoho Catalyst Console
- [ ] Check .env file is in .gitignore
- [ ] Test registration flow end-to-end

---

## 🆘 Quick Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution | Doc |
|-------|----------|-----|
| "KeyError: SECRET_KEY" | Add SECRET_KEY to .env | ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES_GUIDE |
| Keys too short | Regenerate using secrets.token_hex() | ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES_GUIDE |
| Emails not sending | Verify email in Zoho Catalyst | CATALYST_EMAIL_SETUP |
| Session validation fails | Check SESSION_SECRET is set | SESSION_ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE |
| CSRF token invalid | Verify X-CSRF-Token header | SESSION_ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE |

---

## 📌 Important Reminders

🚨 **CRITICAL:**
- Never commit `.env` to Git (add to .gitignore)
- Never share `.env` file
- Generate unique keys for each environment
- Verify email in Zoho Catalyst before using
- Use HTTPS in production

✅ **GOOD PRACTICES:**
- Store keys securely
- Rotate keys periodically
- Use different keys per environment
- Document your configuration
- Keep .env file permissions restricted

---

**Start with ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES_QUICK_REFERENCE.txt → Ready to go! 🚀**
